name: Leptospirosis
description: Leptospirosis is a bacterial infection caused by Leptospira serovars spread through contaminated water and rodent urine. It causes acute kidney injury, liver failure, and systemic inflammation. It is an important zoonosis transmissible to humans.
treatment_overview: Penicillin or amoxicillin followed by doxycycline to clear the carrier state. IV fluids for renal support, anti-nausea drugs, and monitoring of kidney and liver function. Intensive care for severe cases.
prevention_tips: Good hygiene when handling infected dogs
affected_breeds: all breeds (working and sporting dogs with outdoor exposure at higher risk)
